comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Description: Check if engine exports Record-style types for XP or signal tracking

rg -nP --type=ts 'export (type|interface).*\b(XpBySource|XpTotals|SignalTimes|LastSignalAt)\b'
```

Repository: cesarnml/codogotchi

Length of output: 336

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Read the full XpTotals type definition
cat -n packages/engine/src/xp.ts | head -50
```

Repository: cesarnml/codogotchi

Length of output: 1737

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Find and read LootSource definition from the engine
rg -n 'type LootSource|enum LootSource|export.*LootSource' packages/engine --type=ts -A 5
```

Repository: cesarnml/codogotchi

Length of output: 661

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Read the full relevant section of convex/schema.ts to see all definitions and context
cat -n convex/schema.ts | head -60
```

Repository: cesarnml/codogotchi

Length of output: 2556

---



</details>

**Add compile-time validation to xpBySource and lastSignalAtBySource.**

The hardcoded keys in `xpBySource` and `lastSignalAtBySource` must stay synchronized with `lootSources`, but unlike `lootSources`, they lack a `satisfies` check. If `LootSource` changes in the engine, the `satisfies` assertion on `lootSources` will fail and force an update, but `xpBySource` and `lastSignalAtBySource` won't be caught, risking schema drift.

Add `satisfies` assertions to both objects to validate their keys match the `LootSource` union:
```typescript
const xpBySource = v.object({...}) satisfies Record<LootSource, any>;
const lastSignalAtBySource = v.object({...}) satisfies Record<LootSource, any>;
```

This ensures compile-time consistency across all three definitions without requiring engine-side changes.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@convex/schema.ts` around lines 28 - 43, The xpBySource and
lastSignalAtBySource schema objects must be asserted to match the LootSource
union to prevent key drift; update their declarations (xpBySource and
lastSignalAtBySource) to include a TypeScript "satisfies" assertion such as
"satisfies Record<LootSource, any>" so the compiler enforces that their keys
match LootSource (the same union used by lootSources). Ensure you reference the
existing v.object(...) expressions for xpBySource and lastSignalAtBySource and
add the satisfies clause to each declaration, leaving the runtime v.object shape
intact.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:puma -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Standardize timestamp representations across the schema.**

The schema uses inconsistent timestamp types:
- `died_at` and `lastSignalAtBySource.*` fields: `v.string()` (ISO-8601)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `ts`: `v.number()` (Unix timestamps)

The comment on lines 35-37 justifies ISO-8601 strings to preserve timezone semantics and avoid parsing overhead. However, this rationale applies equally to all timestamps. The current inconsistency creates several issues:

1. **Lost timezone information** for `created_at`, `updated_at`, and `ts`
2. **Inconsistent query logic** - different comparison/sorting approaches needed
3. **Potential bugs** when comparing timestamps of different types (e.g., `died_at` vs `updated_at`)
4. **Mixed serialization** - some timestamps need `toISOString()`, others don't




<details>
<summary>♻️ Recommended fix: Standardize on ISO-8601 strings</summary>

```diff
 users: defineTable({
   handle: v.string(),
   profile_id: v.string(),
-  created_at: v.number(),
+  created_at: v.string(),
 }).index("by_handle", ["handle"]),

 profiles: defineTable({
   // ... other fields ...
   died_at: v.union(v.string(), v.null()),
   cause: v.union(v.literal("decay"), v.null()),
   death_count: v.number(),
   last_signal_at_by_source: lastSignalAtBySource,
   config_snapshot: configSnapshot,
-  updated_at: v.number(),
+  updated_at: v.string(),
 })
   .index("by_profile_id", ["profile_id"])
   .index("by_handle", ["handle"]),

 loot_events: defineTable({
   profile_id: v.string(),
   tier: v.union(...lootTiers.map((t) => v.literal(t))),
   name: v.string(),
   source: v.union(...lootSources.map((s) => v.literal(s))),
   score_explanation: v.union(v.string(), v.null()),
-  ts: v.number(),
+  ts: v.string(),
 })
```

This preserves timezone semantics consistently and simplifies query logic.

</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
export default defineSchema({
	users: defineTable({
		handle: v.string(),
		profile_id: v.string(),
		created_at: v.string(),
	}).index("by_handle", ["handle"]),

	profiles: defineTable({
		profile_id: v.string(),
		handle: v.string(),
		xp_by_source: xpBySource,
		total_xp: v.number(),
		stage: v.number(),
		hp: v.number(),
		mood: v.union(...moodLiterals),
		died_at: v.union(v.string(), v.null()),
		// `cause` mirrors engine `ProfileHealth.cause` (`"decay" | undefined`).
		// Persisted as `"decay" | null` so the column is queryable and matches
		// the only failure mode the engine currently emits.
		cause: v.union(v.literal("decay"), v.null()),
		death_count: v.number(),
		last_signal_at_by_source: lastSignalAtBySource,
		config_snapshot: configSnapshot,
		updated_at: v.string(),
	})
		.index("by_profile_id", ["profile_id"])
		.index("by_handle", ["handle"]),

	loot_events: defineTable({
		profile_id: v.string(),
		tier: v.union(...lootTiers.map((t) => v.literal(t))),
		name: v.string(),
		source: v.union(...lootSources.map((s) => v.literal(s))),
		score_explanation: v.union(v.string(), v.null()),
		ts: v.string(),
	})
		.index("by_profile_id", ["profile_id"])
		.index("by_profile_id_ts", ["profile_id", "ts"])
		.index("by_ts", ["ts"]),
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@convex/schema.ts` around lines 58 - 96, The schema mixes Unix numbers and
ISO-8601 strings for timestamps; standardize all timestamps to ISO-8601 strings
by replacing v.number() for created_at, updated_at (in the users and profiles
tables) and ts (in loot_events) with v.string(), and ensure any related
composite types referenced (lastSignalAtBySource, died_at already v.string())
are consistently string-typed so comparisons and serialization are uniform;
update any validation or consumers expecting numeric timestamps accordingly.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:puma -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Agent state is incorrectly marked as completed.**

`agents[*].state = "completed"` and `findingsCount = 0` contradict the embedded vendor messages (rate-limited / paused). This can produce false “clean” automation outcomes.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 	"agents": [
 		{
 			"agent": "coderabbit",
-			"state": "completed",
-			"findingsCount": 0,
-			"note": "review completed without actionable findings"
+			"state": "blocked",
+			"findingsCount": null,
+			"note": "review blocked: vendor rate-limited"
 		},
 		{
 			"agent": "qodo",
-			"state": "completed",
-			"findingsCount": 0,
-			"note": "review completed without actionable findings"
+			"state": "paused",
+			"findingsCount": null,
+			"note": "review paused by vendor eligibility"
 		}
 	],
```
</details>


Also applies to: 25-37

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/reviews/P1.05-ai-review.fetch.json` around
lines 10 - 19, The agent entries currently set agents[*].state = "completed" and
findingsCount = 0 despite embedded vendor messages indicating
rate-limited/paused status; update the JSON so the state field reflects the
actual vendor condition (e.g., "rate_limited" or "paused") and adjust
findingsCount (non-zero or null) and note to accurately represent
incomplete/paused reviews; apply the same change to the other agent blocks
referenced (lines 25-37) and ensure any consumer logic that checks
agents[*].state can handle the new status values.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Repository artifact includes tenant/account identifier in plain text.**

The stored comment body contains a billing URL with a concrete `tenantId` value. Even if not a secret, this is account-identifying metadata and should be sanitized before commit.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-.../subscription?tab=usage&tenantId=f97770c6-815f-4b45-bf56-988a5a13acaa
+.../subscription?tab=usage&tenantId=<redacted>
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/reviews/P1.05-ai-review.fetch.json` at line
25, The stored comment's "body" field contains a concrete tenantId query
parameter (tenantId=...) that exposes account-identifying metadata; update the
stored JSON and the comment-generation path that produces this payload to
sanitize that parameter: in the JSON "body" entry remove or replace the tenantId
value with a placeholder (e.g. [REDACTED]) and modify the comment producer (the
code that builds the comment string / URL) to strip or obfuscate any "tenantId"
query param before persisting or emitting comments so future comments never
include raw tenant identifiers.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Triage outcome should not be `clean` when reviews were blocked/paused.**

Given the fetch artifact evidence (rate-limited and paused vendors), this record overstates review completion and can incorrectly pass governance checks.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-	"outcome": "clean",
-	"note": "External AI review completed without prudent follow-up changes.",
+	"outcome": "incomplete",
+	"note": "External AI review incomplete: one vendor rate-limited, one vendor paused.",
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
	"outcome": "incomplete",
	"note": "External AI review incomplete: one vendor rate-limited, one vendor paused.",
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/reviews/P1.05-ai-review.triage.json` around
lines 5 - 6, The "outcome" field is incorrectly set to "clean" despite evidence
of paused/blocked vendor reviews; change the "outcome" value from "clean" to a
status reflecting incomplete review (e.g., "blocked" or "paused") and update the
"note" field (currently "External AI review completed without prudent follow-up
changes.") to explicitly state that reviews were rate-limited/paused and require
follow-up, referencing the fetch artifact evidence; ensure you modify the JSON
keys "outcome" and "note" so the record no longer misrepresents completion.
```

</details>

<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/cesarnml/codogotchi/pull/7?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

The PR implements a complete Convex database schema for the codogotchi application. The schema defines three tables with compile-time type validation: a `users` table storing profiles, a `profiles` table capturing player state (XP, health, mood, death metadata) with per-source signal tracking and persisted config snapshots, and a `loot_events` table recording tier/source/score events. Mood states and loot enums are validated at compile-time via `satisfies` assertions against engine types to catch drift. Supporting documentation explains the type-safety rationale and implementation deferments.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> docs/product/delivery/phase-01/ticket-06-convex-schema.md (1)
> > `14-14`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **Resolve contradictory statements about `convex dev --typecheck`.** > > Line 14 states "`convex dev --typecheck` passes locally," but lines 55-59 explain that the CLI is deferred to P1.07/P1.08 because it requires Cloud bootstrap. These statements contradict each other. > > > > > >
> 📝 Proposed fix > > If typecheck is deferred, remove or revise line 14: > > ```diff > - Indexes: `users.handle`, `profiles.profile_id`, `loot_events.profile_id` + `loot_events.ts`. > -- `convex dev --typecheck` passes locally. > - No mutations defined yet — schema only. `syncProfile` lands in P1.07. > ``` > > Or clarify: > > ```diff > -- `convex dev --typecheck` passes locally. > +- Schema typechecks via `bun run ci:quiet`. `convex dev --typecheck` deferred to P1.07. > ``` > >
> > > Also applies to: 55-59 > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-01/ticket-06-convex-schema.md` at line 14, Update > the contradictory statements about "convex dev --typecheck": either remove the > standalone assertion "`convex dev --typecheck` passes locally" or revise it to > explicitly state the conditional (e.g., "convex dev --typecheck passes locally > when Cloud bootstrap is available; otherwise the CLI/typecheck is deferred to > P1.07/P1.08"). Also update the later section that defers the CLI (the paragraph > referencing P1.07/P1.08 and Cloud bootstrap) to match the chosen wording so both > places consistently state whether local typechecking is supported or deferred. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
convex/schema.ts (2)
`6-9`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Clarify the drift protection mechanism.** The comment states "keep this list locked to HP_OVERLAY_STATES via the `satisfies` assertion," but the satisfies check on line 9 doesn't lock or detect drift—it only validates that the imported constant conforms to the expected type. If `HP_OVERLAY_STATES` changes in `@codogotchi/contracts`, `moodLiterals` automatically reflects the change. This differs from `lootTiers`/`lootSources` (lines 14-26), where hardcoded literals with satisfies assertions do provide compile-time drift detection. Consider revising the comment to clarify that mood derives directly from contracts and intentionally tracks changes, unlike loot enums which are mirrored with drift gates.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@convex/schema.ts` around lines 6 - 9, The comment for moodLiterals is misleading: the satisfies check on HP_OVERLAY_STATES only validates its type and does not "lock" the list against upstream changes, so moodLiterals will automatically follow any changes in HP_OVERLAY_STATES from `@codogotchi/contracts`; update the comment above moodLiterals (and mention HP_OVERLAY_STATES and moodLiterals) to state that this value is intentionally derived from the contracts and will track contract changes, and contrast that with lootTiers/lootSources which are hardcoded here with satisfies assertions to act as compile-time drift gates. ```
--- `45-56`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Add type validation for `configSnapshot`.** The comment states this mirrors `HealthConfig` from the engine, but unlike `lootTiers`/`lootSources`, there's no `satisfies` assertion to enforce compile-time consistency. If `HealthConfig` changes in the engine, this schema won't detect the drift.
🛡️ Proposed fix to add type validation If the engine exports `HealthConfig`, add a satisfies assertion: ```diff +import type { HealthConfig } from "`@codogotchi/engine`"; const configSnapshot = v.object({ weekend_decay: v.boolean(), grace_days: v.number(), vacation_until: v.union(v.string(), v.null()), timezone: v.string(), decay_per_day: v.number(), revive_threshold: v.number(), revive_hp: v.number(), -}); +}) satisfies ConvexValidator; ``` (Adjust the satisfies pattern to match Convex's validator type system if needed.)
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@convex/schema.ts` around lines 45 - 56, Add a compile-time type assertion so the Convex schema configSnapshot is validated against the engine's HealthConfig type: import HealthConfig from the engine and assert that configSnapshot satisfies the validator type for HealthConfig (similar to how lootTiers/lootSources are asserted) so any shape changes to HealthConfig produce a type error; update the declaration around configSnapshot to include this satisfies-style check referencing configSnapshot and HealthConfig and adjust imports/types to match the Convex validator types. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@convex/schema.ts`: - Around line 58-96: The schema mixes Unix numbers and ISO-8601 strings for timestamps; standardize all timestamps to ISO-8601 strings by replacing v.number() for created_at, updated_at (in the users and profiles tables) and ts (in loot_events) with v.string(), and ensure any related composite types referenced (lastSignalAtBySource, died_at already v.string()) are consistently string-typed so comparisons and serialization are uniform; update any validation or consumers expecting numeric timestamps accordingly. - Around line 28-43: The xpBySource and lastSignalAtBySource schema objects must be asserted to match the LootSource union to prevent key drift; update their declarations (xpBySource and lastSignalAtBySource) to include a TypeScript "satisfies" assertion such as "satisfies Record" so the compiler enforces that their keys match LootSource (the same union used by lootSources). Ensure you reference the existing v.object(...) expressions for xpBySource and lastSignalAtBySource and add the satisfies clause to each declaration, leaving the runtime v.object shape intact.
